abstract: In this paper, we introduce BEAST 2, an open-source and modular software environment designed for Bayesian evolutionary inference. While built upon the foundation of its predecessor, BEAST 1, this new platform represents a comprehensive architectural redesign aimed at overcoming technical limitations in extensibility and long-term maintenance. A primary innovation of BEAST 2 is its integrated package management system, which empowers the research community to develop and distribute independent modules. This allows for the seamless integration of new evolutionary models and statistical methods directly into the software ecosystem without requiring core updates. We demonstrate the versatility of this framework by highlighting several newly implemented models, including advanced birth-death processes, phylodynamic tools, and multi-locus species tree inference methods. BEAST 2 provides a robust, future-proof platform for the development of sophisticated phylogenetics-based analyses.
